limits.metaDescription: A calculator for all types of limits, including a step-by-step solution process.

calculator.heading: Limits Calculation Engine
calculator.hint: Enter the limit expressions using x as the variable. The denominator is optional.

messages.nerdamerFailed: Nerdamer could not resolve the limit symbolically.
messages.oscillationDetected: The function oscillates infinitely near the point.
messages.oscillationNoLHopital: Oscillation detected (e.g. sin(1/x)). L'Hôpital not applied.
messages.bothLateralsEqual: Both lateral limits match → Bilateral limit = {value}
messages.lateralsDifferent: The lateral limits are different or the function oscillates. Bilateral limit does not exist.
messages.detectedIndet: Detected indeterminate form {type}. Applying L'Hôpital's rule.
messages.numeratorNotZeroDenZero: Numerator is not 0 and denominator tends to 0 → Infinity.

relations.metaDescription: Tool to analyze properties of binary relations: reflexive, symmetric, transitive, etc.

errors.noPairs: No valid pairs found. Use format {(A,B),(C,D)}

reflexive.title: Reflexive:
reflexive.desc: For every x in the base set, (x,x) is in the relation. Example: base {A,B}, relation contains (A,A) and (B,B).

irreflexive.title: Irreflexive:
irreflexive.desc: No (x,x) belongs to the relation. Example: relation (A,B) without (A,A) nor (B,B).

symmetric.title: Symmetric:
symmetric.desc: If (a,b) is present then (b,a) is also present. Example: (A,B),(B,A).

asymmetric.title: Asymmetric:
asymmetric.desc: There are no (x,x) and if (a,b) with a≠b then (b,a) is not present. Example: (A,B) without (B,A).

antisymmetric.title: Antisymmetric:
antisymmetric.desc: If (a,b) and (b,a) exist then a=b. Example: (A,A),(B,B) satisfies it.

transitive.title: Transitive:
transitive.desc: If (a,b) and (b,c) exist then (a,c) must exist. Example: (A,B),(B,C),(A,C).

syntaxGuide.title: Syntax guide
syntaxGuide.formatExplanation: Write the relation as a list of ordered pairs inside braces. Valid examples:
rules: Separate pairs with commas, wrap everything in braces: { ... }.
Extra spaces are allowed. Symbols are case-sensitive.
syntaxGuide.advice: Tip: Use simple letters for base set elements (A, B, C...), or numbers if you prefer.

syntaxGuideLimits.title: Usage guide
syntaxGuideLimits.formatExplanation: Write mathematical expressions using standard syntax. Some valid examples:
rules: For natural log use log(x) (not ln(x)).
syntaxGuideLimits.advice: Tip: If you get an unexpected result, check the expression syntax and correct use of functions like log(), sqrt() or exp().

typesLimits.title: Supported limit types
typesLimits.limitAtPointDesc: They calculate the value the function approaches when the variable x gets closer to a specific number.
typesLimits.limitAtInfinityDesc: They analyze the behavior of the function to understand what happens when x increases or decreases without bound towards ±∞.
typesLimits.finiteLimitsDesc: These occur when, upon evaluating the trend, the result of the limit stabilizes at an exact and defined real number.
typesLimits.infiniteLimitsDesc: These occur when the function has no bound and its value increases or decreases indefinitely towards ±∞ as it approaches a specific point.
typesLimits.oneSidedLimitsDesc: They evaluate the behavior of the function by separating the approach from the left and from the right. They are essential to confirm whether the general limit exists.
typesLimits.indeterminateLimitsDesc: They detect and solve mathematical cases without a direct solution (such as the 0/0 or ∞/∞ forms), applying advanced methods like L'Hôpital's rule.

relationsUtils.noValidPairs: No valid pairs found. Use format {(A,B),(C,D)}
